The natural language proof describes the standard approach:
1. For any ε > 0, define U_n = {x ∈ [a,b] | f x - f_n x < ε}

5. By compactness of [a,b], there exists N such that [a,b] ⊆ U_N
6. This gives uniform convergence

node.natural_language_proof:
limit on `[a,b]` is uniform.

Proof: fix `eps > 0` and let `K = [a,b]`. For each `n`, set
`U_n = {x in K | f x - f_n n x < eps}`. Since `f` and `f_n n` are continuous on `K`, each `U_n` is
open in `K`. Monotonicity in `n` implies `U_n ⊆ U_{n+1}`. Also pointwise convergence implies that
for every `x ∈ K` we have `f_n n x -> f x`, so eventually `f x - f_n n x < eps`, hence
`⋃ n, U_n = K`.
Because `K` is compact, an increasing open cover has a single set that already covers: there is
`N` with `U_N = K`. Thus for all `x ∈ K`, `0 ≤ f x - f_n N x < eps`, which is exactly uniform
convergence on `K`.

declaration.natural_language: Given a uniform space β, a sequence of functions \(F_n : \alpha \to \beta\), a candidate limit function \(f : \alpha \to \beta\), a filter \(p\) on the index type, and a set \(s \subseteq \alpha\), this defines “\(F_n\) converges uniformly on \(s\) to \(f\) with respect to \(p\)”. Concretely, for every entourage \(u\) of the diagonal in \(\beta\), there is a \(p\)-eventual set of indices \(n\) such that for all \(x \in s\), the pair \((f(x), F_n(x))\) lies in \(u\).

node.natural_language_proof:
limit on `[a,b]` is uniform.

Proof: fix `eps > 0` and let `K = [a,b]`. For each `n`, set
`U_n = {x in K | f x - f_n n x < eps}`. Since `f` and `f_n n` are continuous on `K`, each `U_n` is
open in `K`. Monotonicity in `n` implies `U_n ⊆ U_{n+1}`. Also pointwise convergence implies that
for every `x ∈ K` we have `f_n n x -> f x`, so eventually `f x - f_n n x < eps`, hence
`⋃ n, U_n = K`.
Because `K` is compact, an increasing open cover has a single set that already covers: there is
